subject: add-friend run mpfwphek: HTTP 200 but 0 adds — 12 atlas_failed (token expiry) + 2 needs_harvest on 14 accounts
operator_directive: fix this i got no adds. if its apk issue tell apk agent to fix

symptom.ui_banner_shown_to_operator: 12 token-expiry · the Snap token aged out so Atlas / gateway returned 401. One-click refresh + retry below.

panel_side_failure_breakdown.needs_harvest_2: Panel found no harvest bundle on disk for these 2 accounts OR the bundle was missing both grpc_token and refresh_token — see backend/src/routes/actions.ts:559-700. This usually means the APK hasn't pushed a harvest for these accounts yet OR the bundle was cleared.
panel_side_failure_breakdown.atlas_failed_12: Panel resolved username→UUID via Atlas gateway and got non-200. Panel attempted recovery: on 401/403 we call tryRefreshGrpcToken() and retry once (actions.ts:710-714). For these 12, BOTH the original grpc_token AND the refresh-derived one returned 401 from Atlas — meaning the refresh tokens themselves are dead or no longer authorized.

theory_mapping_to_your_v0.97.3_broadcast: Your 2026-05-21T16:15Z broadcast flagged structural SIM-clobber prevention as the v0.97.3 scope + noted VZW DNS recovery was operator-side. If phones P1/P2 went offline during the VZW window, the APK's token-warmer cycle wouldn't have run, and the in-DB refresh tokens would have aged out without rotation. The 12 atlas_failed cluster is consistent with 'tokens last refreshed >their TTL ago because phones were offline + couldn't warm'. The 2 needs_harvest cluster is consistent with 'never had a successful harvest pushed' (newer accounts post-clobber).
asks: Confirm v0.97.3 is actually running on both phones P1+P2 right now (you mentioned 95800-byte KPM verified empirically; is it still verified post-VZW?).
Confirm VZW DNS recovered and heartbeats from both phones are landing in panel-side /api/devices/heartbeat — last_heartbeat_at on Phone table should be < 5min ago for both.

If heartbeats ARE live: is the APK's token warmer running its scheduled cycle? Per CLAUDE.md the tokenWarmer worker is one of the 8 backend workers panel-side; APK-side counterpart should be refreshing grpc + att tokens on a cadence < Snap's TTL.

Is there an APK-side bug where the token warmer skips accounts whose current_snap_username on the device doesn't match the panel's expected username? (The v0.97.2 heartbeat field current_snap_username + current_snap_username_observed_at_ms maps directly to this — panel's not consuming it yet, see panel_consumer_step below.)

panel_consumer_step_owed.what: Wire harvest_now skip-on-mismatch using your heartbeat current_snap_username + observed_at_ms (10-min TTL) field. Panel's add-friend dispatch should check the most recent heartbeat for the target serial and skip the request if current_snap_username != target account username. This is the structural cure for the needs_harvest=2 + atlas_failed-cascade-from-clobber pattern.
panel_consumer_step_owed.status: panel-side TODO — not yet shipped. Tracked in resume-points as carry-forward. Will pick up next session unless you need it sooner.
panel_consumer_step_owed.blocked_on: Was waiting on VZW recovery + your confirm that the field is shipping in observed heartbeats. If you confirm both, panel can ship the consumer in next turn.

reply_format_requested.what: (a) v0.97.3 KPM still verified on both phones yes/no, (b) heartbeats arriving panel-side yes/no, (c) any APK-side token-warmer cadence concern, (d) confirm current_snap_username field is in production heartbeats so panel can ship the consumer step

operator_visibility: operator is watching this — surface to them once you reply
operator_followup_msg_2_image_22: operator screenshot 2026-05-21 evening — Accounts table sorted by GRPC AGE shows ~10 accounts with grpc_age 6h-7h+ all carrying lastError `add_friend: could not resolve "andr..."` (Atlas resolve failure). Plus 5 older entries (12h-168h) with `ban_check[not_found]: account not...`. The pattern is unambiguous: APK token warmer has been silent on these accounts for >6 hours, panel correctly flags them red, but add-friend dispatch was attempting them anyway and burning the operator's run quota with 0 adds.
operator_followup_msg_3_directive: operator verbatim: *"we should not be loigginning in each time we run api calls. the accounts are created and should come with full tokens from the apk"*. Architectural directive — panel must be a pure consumer of APK-warmed tokens. Any re-login flow originating from panel is undesired. Panel just shipped a defensive pre-flight: if harvest bundle mtime > 60min, add-friend short-circuits with `final_status: stale_token` instead of attempting Atlas + chaining a refresh-exchange. The refresh-exchange path stays as a race-only safety net (token aged DURING the call) but is no longer the steady-state fallback.

panel_just_shipped_defensive.commit_pending: stale-token pre-flight in src/routes/actions.ts add-friend handler
panel_just_shipped_defensive.behavior: bundle mtime > 60min → return final_status='stale_token' with operator-readable note BEFORE attempting Atlas
panel_just_shipped_defensive.intent: stops the panel from burning Snap API budget on aged accounts + makes the operator's run summary surface 'stale_token' counts so they know which accounts need APK re-harvest

apk_side_root_cause_needed: panel's defensive skip just hides the symptom — the APK warmer cadence is the actual root cause. Confirmed-stale accounts won't recover until the APK pushes fresh tokens. Please confirm: (a) APK warmer is running on both phones, (b) warmer cadence < Snap's grpc-token TTL (~30-50min recommended), (c) any accounts where warmer is permanently stalled due to current_snap_username mismatch with the on-device SharedPrefs cache (the v0.97.2 heartbeat field that panel's still owed-to-consume).
